subject: Improve Ansible execution performance with Mitogen
commit_message:
Improve Ansible execution performance with Mitogen

Issue-ID: OOM-1919

Signed-off-by: Samuli Silvius <s.silvius@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: Ic875e74eb4df81a1d3f6a36cad4f9070db4ac16e

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/387/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/387

Patch Set 1:

1. Can you share some benchmark results? Commit msg claims improvement but i think we need to witness some numbers.

2. What is the gain compared to enabling 'pipelining' that is currently disabled?

3. Mitogen supports only Ansible up to 2.7, 2.8 is the current and we'll probably want to upgrade in near future, that will stop us.

4. Did you do some thoughtful testing to prove there is no negative impact in any offline-installer code corner? Docs mention some speed downs while using "delegate_to" to localhost, which is the case for us.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> 1. Can you share some benchmark results? Commit msg claims improvement but i think we need to witness some numbers.
> 
> 2. What is the gain compared to enabling 'pipelining' that is currently disabled?
> 
> 3. Mitogen supports only Ansible up to 2.7, 2.8 is the current and we'll probably want to upgrade in near future, that will stop us.
> 
> 4. Did you do some thoughtful testing to prove there is no negative impact in any offline-installer code corner? Docs mention some speed downs while using "delegate_to" to localhost, which is the case for us.

Feels like committing to new project nobody knows me. Good however to expect any committer is a student level:)

1. No

2. It's faster

3. It does not stop us. And it can always disabled. Do you think Mitogen development is dead and no support for Ansible versions follow?

4. NO. I know those. Do you expect each committer has done throughful testing of each commit? And this is particular special one.


Before you comment on WIP, will change it to WIP. 
Technically it works, but this needs to be tested with full cycle with offline-installer and check if any problems occur and more over if perf benefits happens.
